raw_data.details: The Department of Distance & Education, School of Open Learning, Campus of Open Learning (DDCE/SOL/COL), in the spirit of facilitating financially weak students as per guidelines decided by the University of Delhi, has announced the Financial Support Scheme for DDCE/SOL/COL students. This scheme is reflected through a fee waiver/refund for the students enrolled in DDCE/SOL/COL and having their family income below the poverty line. The fee waiver/refund includes all components of the fee paid by students except the examination fee and the university fee.
raw_data.benefits: S. No. Family income (in ₹) per annumFee waiver (percentage)RemarksCategory 1Less than or equal to ₹4,00,000/Up to 100%Actual fee or up to a maximum of ₹15000Category 2Between ₹4,00,000 and ₹8,00,000Up to 50%Actual fee or up to a maximum of ₹10,000
raw_data.eligibility: A full-time bonafide student studying in any UG/PG Program in a department/institute/center of the University of Delhi (UoD). The family income of the student should be less than ₹8,00,000. Students enrolled in constituent colleges of UoD. B. Tech Programs (excluding CIC) Five-year integrated LAW programs, SoL and NCWEB are NOT eligible.
raw_data.exclusions: 2nd, 3rd & 4th year candidates with ER/Arrears of previous examination papers are not eligible to apply.
raw_data.application_process:
Online
Step 1: Eligible students can fill in the online [application form](https://fss.oldcdu.ac.in/) available on the website of the Dean's Students’ Welfare Office.
Step 2: Students must submit a copy of their application form and all documents after due attestation and recommendation from the head/director of the concerned department/center/institute at DSW office.
Step 3: The schedule for the submission of the hard/printed copy of the application form will be declared in the DSW website. Helpdesk:For any queries kindly contact: fss@dsw.du.ac.inOffice Address:Dean Students’ Welfare,Conference Centre (Gate No. 4),Opposite Botany Department,University of Delhi,Delhi - 110007
raw_data.documents_required: Copy of EWS/OBC-NCL Certificate or Annual Family Income Certificate issued by competent authority. (Income Certificate signed by a notary will not be accepted). Self-attested copies of latest income Tax Returns (2022-23) of the following family members:Father,Mother,Sister(s)(Unmarried),Brother(s) (Unmarried and below 25 years of age),Copy of PAN card(s) of all the above. Signed copy of the Undertaking Form. Copy of Bonafide Certificate signed by the Head/Director of the Centre/Department/ Institute. Copy of the mark sheet of the last exam passed. Copy of the latest Fee Receipt. Copy of Bank Pass Book showing the student's name, Account number and IFSC code or a cancelled cheque.

faqs.question: I am pursuing an Undergraduate Course in a Constituent College/ School of Open Learning/ Non Collegiate Women Education Board of UoD. Am I eligible to apply?
faqs.answer: Students studying in any Constituent College/ School of Open Learning/ Non-Collegiate Women Education Board of UoD are NOT eligible to apply for the FSS. FSS is only for those students who have deposited their fees directly to UoD and NOT to any Constituent College/ School of Open Learning/ Non-Collegiate Women Education Board.

faqs.question: I am a First Year/ Semester student. Can I apply?
faqs.answer: Any student who is pursuing a regular Undergraduate/ Postgraduate Degree Program at a Department/ Institution/ Centre of University of Delhi (UoD), including First Year/ Semester students can apply.

faqs.question: I am doing a Certificate/Diploma/Advanced Diploma language course. Am I eligible for FSS?
faqs.answer: Only full-time bonafide students pursuing a regular Undergraduate/ Postgraduate Degree at a Department/ Institution/ Centre of University of Delhi / are eligible for FSS.

faqs.question: I am pursuing a B. Tech. Program under the Faculty of Technology. Am I eligible for FSS?
faqs.answer: UG students pursuing B. Tech. Programs (Computer Science & Engineering; Electronics & Computer Engineering; Electrical Engineering) under Faculty of Technology are not eligible to apply under this scheme as they have already been granted fee waiver during the time of admission.

faqs.question: I am pursuing a Five Year Integrated Law Program (B. A. LLB./ BBA. LL. B). Am I eligible to apply for FSS?
faqs.answer: UG students pursuing a Five Year Integrated Law Program (B. A. LLB./ BBA. LL. B) are NOT eligible to apply under this scheme as they have already been granted fee waiver during the time of admission.

faqs.question: I have an Essential Repeat (ER) paper in my previous examination. Can I still apply?
faqs.answer: Students with Essential Repeat papers in the previous examination are NOT eligible to apply.

faqs.question: Will this scheme also include the examination fees/ hostel fees?
faqs.answer: The fee waiver includes all components of the fee paid by students except examination fee and hostel fee.

faqs.question: My family does not file Income Tax Return (ITR). Am I eligible to apply?
faqs.answer: For such students it is mandatory to submit a copy of EWS/ OBC-NCL Certificate or Annual family income certificate issued by competent authority after March 31, 2023. Income certificates issued by a notary will not be accepted.

faqs.question: What do I upload in the Income Tax section as my family does not file ITR?
faqs.answer: Students must upload the Undertaking Form (signed copy) in place of the ITR.

faqs.question: Where do I submit my application?
faqs.answer: Students must submit a copy of their Application Form and all documents after due attestation and recommendation from the Head/ Director of the concerned Department/ Centre/ Institute as per schedule declared at DSW website.
